data.title: Coterra Energy to discuss Q4 & full year 2025 results on Feb 27, 2026, after market closure. Conference call at 9 AM CT.
data.description: In Houston, Coterra Energy Inc. (NYSE: CTRA) will be holding a conference call on February 27, 2026, at 9:00 AM CT to review its financial and operating performance for the fourth quarter and full year of 2025. The results for this period will be officially disclosed after the market closes on February 26, 2026. Interested parties can join the conference call at 10:00 AM ET.

data.title: Cavco Industries reported Q3 fiscal results, $581.0 million revenue, 343 homes sold by American Homestar, $44 million in stock repurchases. CEO optimistic on market outlook.
data.description: Cavco Industries, Inc. announced their financial results for the third fiscal quarter ended on December 27, 2025. They reported a net revenue of $581.0 million, up by 11.3% from the previous year, with home sales volume up by 3.2%. Gross profit percentages for factory-built housing and financial services were 21.7% and 65.2%, respectively. Income before income taxes was $58 million, down 16.9% from the previous year. American Homestar contributed $42 million to the revenue. Backlogs totaled $160 million. Stock repurchases were $44 million. The company's CEO mentioned optimistic market outlook and positive impact expected from the American Homestar acquisition.

data.title: GSI Technology, Inc. reported 12% revenue growth in Q3 FY2026 to $6.1 million, with a cash balance of $70.7 million.
data.description: GSI Technology reported financial results for its third fiscal quarter ended December 31, 2025. Revenue increased by 12% year-over-year to $6.1 million. At the end of the quarter, the company had a cash balance of $70.7 million, up from $13.4 million in the previous quarter. They announced a proof-of-concept with G2 Tech and two government agencies, expecting around $1 million in government funding. The company's Gemini-II achieved fast performance metrics compared to GPU-based systems. Operating expenses were $10.1 million, while net loss for the quarter was $3.0 million. GSI Technology focused on pursuing initial design wins in defense and commercial edge deployments. The company's stockholders' equity was $83.6 million as of December 31, 2025. The earnings conference call was scheduled for January 29, 2026. GSI Technology is positioned well in edge computing and AI solutions with their Gemini-I and Gemini-II technologies. For more details, visit their website at www.gsitechnology.com.

data.title: Vaxcyte plans a public offering of $500 million common stock and pre-funded warrants, with a 30-day option for $75 million more.
data.description: Vaxcyte, Inc., a vaccine innovation company, headquartered in San Carlos, California, has announced the initiation of an underwritten public offering. The offering consists of $500,000,000 worth of its common stock and pre-funded warrants. Vaxcyte will be offering all the shares of common stock and pre-funded warrants. Additionally, there is a provision for the underwriters to have a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional $75,000,000 of shares of its common stock included in the public offering. However, the completion and final size of the offering are subject to market conditions and other factors, with no guarantee of its completion or the exact terms of the offering.

data.title: Credit Acceptance Corp. reported Q4 2025 net income of $122.0M, $10.99 per share; adjusted net income was $126.0M, $11.35 per share.
data.description: Southfield, Michigan, on January 29, 2026 - Credit Acceptance Corporation (Nasdaq: CACC) disclosed a consolidated net income of $122.0 million, equivalent to $10.99 per diluted share, for the quarter concluding on December 31, 2025. Adjusted net income, a non-GAAP financial metric, for the same period was reported at $126.0 million, or $11.35 per diluted share. This financial information was shared by the company today without specifying the source.

data.title: Colliers acquires Western Canadian engineering services firm, entering energy sector and boosting presence in Alberta.
data.description: Expands presence in Alberta by entering energy sector, contributing to increased scale in the region.

data.title: Senseonics gets CE Mark approval for Eversense 365, the world's longest-lasting CGM; helps diabetes patients.
data.description: In Germantown, Maryland on January 29, 2026, Senseonics Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ: SENS), a medical technology firm specializing in long-term, implantable continuous glucose monitoring systems for individuals with diabetes, disclosed that their Eversense® 365 CGM system has received CE Mark approval. This certification enables the company to market and distribute the CGM system in Europe.

data.title: Coveo reports third quarter fiscal 2026 SaaS subscription revenue of $36.6 million, surpassing guidance with record new business bookings in Gen AI and Commerce offerings.
data.description: Coveo reported a SaaS subscription revenue of $36.6 million, surpassing the predicted figures. The company also achieved record new business bookings and observed robust performance in their Gen AI and Commerce services, attracting both new and loyal customers. Coveo's financial reports are in U.S. dollars and comply with the International Financial Reporting Standards.

data.title: Standex reports Q2 FY26 sales increased by 16.6% YOY to $221.3 million. New product sales grew by ~13% and sales in fast growth markets contributed ~28% of total sales. Quarterly sales increased organically by 6.4% YOY, with electronics up by 11.1% organically. Record quarterly order intake.
data.description: In the second quarter of the fiscal year 2026, sales saw a year-over-year increase of 16.6% to reach $221.3 million. New product sales experienced growth of approximately 13%, whereas sales in rapidly growing markets made up about 28% of the total sales. Organically, sales in the same period rose by 6.4% year over year, with the electronics sector seeing an 11.1% increase. The quarter also marked a record-high order intake with the book to bill ratio...

data.title: High Tide reports record revenue and adjusted EBITDA in Q4 2025. Canadian Cabana Club members exceed 2.5 million, up 45% YoY.
data.description: Throughout the fiscal year, the Company maintained positive free cash flow and saw robust same-store sales growth, solidifying its position in the German medical cannabis market. The number of Canadian Cabana Club members has now surpassed 2.5 million, representing a 45% increase compared to the previous year, the quickest growth rate in the past four quarters.
